prTitle: feat: broadcast WebSocket game_state_update after actions and phase transitions
prBody:
## Summary

Integrates `game_state_update` WebSocket broadcasts into all state-changing REST endpoints so connected clients can refresh their UI in real time without polling.

### Changes

- **`game_broadcast.py`**: Added `game_state_update_message()` builder that includes optional full `GameState` payload
- **`games.py`**: Added `_build_game_state_payload()` helper to serialize current game state (game, players, leaderboard) for broadcasts. Added `game_state_update` broadcasts after:
  - Game start (`POST /games/{id}/start`) — waiting → development
  - Development completed (`POST /games/{id}/countries/{cid}/develop`) — so other players see progress
  - Phase transition (development → actions) — when all players complete development
  - Action performed (`POST /games/{id}/countries/{cid}/actions`) — updated player state after buy_bond, build_bank, etc.
  - Round advance / game completion (`POST /games/{id}/next-round`) — actions → development or completed
- **Tests**: Added 2 new tests for `game_state_update_message` (with and without game_state payload)
- **Docs**: Moved `game_state_update` from "Reserved/Future" to active game state events, updated architecture description to reflect async `broadcast_event`, updated broadcasting flow documentation

### Review Feedback Addressed

- **CRITICAL (async broadcast_event)**: Already fixed in master — `broadcast_event` is async and awaits `_broadcast` directly
- **Unused Optional import**: Was already cleaned up; re-added intentionally for the new `game_state_update_message` signature

### How It Works

The frontend `useGameWebSocket` hook already handles `game_state_update` messages — when the message includes `game_state`, it applies it directly to React state; otherwise it falls back to a REST refetch. By broadcasting full game state after every action and phase transition, all connected clients see updates immediately.

Closes #37
